round2_improvement_notes.linguistic: Synthesized cross-agent evidence (regulatory and botanics) to refine confidence and eliminate non-Anglosphere LHT countries.
round2_improvement_notes.landscape: Reinforced initial findings by incorporating regulatory details (delineator posts) from other agents.
round2_improvement_notes.botanics: Aligned botanical evidence with regulatory and landscape findings to confirm the specific Australian context.
round2_improvement_notes.regulatory: Incorporated meta-agent evidence regarding delineator posts to strengthen the case for Australia over New Zealand.
round2_improvement_notes.meta: Corroborated the specific delineator post detail with linguistic and regulatory findings to form a consistent profile.

verdict.judge_synthesis_notes: The judge provided a clear, evidence-based synthesis. It correctly identified the country as Australia by weighing the convergence of linguistic, landscape, botanical, regulatory, and meta evidence. It explicitly ruled out alternative candidates (US, Canada, UK, NZ) based on specific visual contradictions (LHT, vegetation type, signage style).
